Alright guys, welcome back. So this is module 5 and by this point in the course you've already brought your prospects onto the journey and you've really challenged their beliefs and under your leadership together you've lovingly dissected and disassembled many of those major reasons why they haven't achieved the outcomes they're looking for and in doing so you've really built trust. You've accurately reflected back their world, their context and offered a more encouraging and even more importantly a fixable diagnosis. So now with all that said it's time to prescribe. So the launch as we're using it in this course is that initial moment where your product is now available for sale and whereas after a few days or as much as 10 days in some cases of building anticipation in your pre-launch and coaching them to the point where your product will be a natural fit into their new context and new reality with all the shifted beliefs and reframes it's now time to make that introduction. So I like to call this the tipping point you know and if you look at our process map here you'll see that the launch is where it starts to hit that downward arc. We start using the forces of gravity that we've built up and that force really comes from the new context and new beliefs, the new attitudes and new feelings they have about their problem about you, about what they're trying to achieve. So this is where we have that downward momentum. It's a lot more difficult to build up new beliefs to have them release a lot of their old attitudes and that's why we do it on that first part in that path of less friction. You know there's friction once there's a product so that's why we do all that ground work in that place of lesser friction and at this point now we introduce that product where it could be this downward slope and we want to really capitalize on that momentum and bring your prospect across that buying line with a powerful launch sequence. So the way I like to equate the launch you know I keep this image in front of me actually on my desktop when I'm writing the launch day emails and it's just these waves you know waves are peaceful they're natural but at the same time they're a very very powerful force you know and at this point we want to ride the momentum of the current we want our prospect to ride the momentum of that current because you really can't swim against it so there is this sense of ease and naturalness to it in allowing those waves to just carry you to that shore but at the same time swimming against it could be this very violent and even dangerous and just not a good situation so we really want to capitalize on that and give them the chance to ride that moment momentum in a very natural way instead of resisting it. So even though this is a really short sequence either one or two emails there's actually quite a lot we need to be aware of and quite a few mistakes that marketers make that not only sabotage sales at the beginning of the launch but also make it much more difficult to sell and convert later as well so we're going to dive into a complete breakdown of exactly what we're coaching here so first and foremost what this sequence is coaching is progress and sustained momentum and this is exactly what I was just alluding to the goal is to really reflect back to them how far they've come how far they've traveled and how the cost of going backwards would really be going against the grain there's more resistance in that there's more risk in that so we're really actually creating a sense of loss aversion around momentum itself you know we talk so much about loss aversion around tangible items but like we reference in module three loss aversion could also be with your exclusive empowerment privilege points no one wants to lose out on that chance to capitalize on it and the same applies to the momentum they've generated no one wants to lose their momentum and the way we do this is by also selling your prospect on the fact that Rome is still burning you know if for a second you imagine that the journey we're taking them on this from this locked door within a burning building and we unlocked it for them and then show them a new path and helped clear the smoke and sheltered them from those flames and we finally brought them to this fire escape and all they have to do is step out take one step instead of going back into that burning building that is what we want to create here and where marketers get this terribly wrong is that they mistakenly try to create urgency here by dropping a time deadline you know some people say only open for five days and ironically the only thing that that's selling is procrastination is sense that we can wait that the flames we were running from and trying to escape aren't as threatening as we thought that after all that running and climbing we can afford now to just take a break rest our feet chill out and take a few breaths so when we try to use time urgency too early you know anywhere before the 48 hour mark really what we're really doing is putting out the fire for them and giving them a reason to procrastinate you know dousing the flames of desire and urgency by giving them permission to kind of chill so what we really want to do is communicate that Rome is still burning so instead that's really what we want to do and that's what keeping their best interest in mind will really dictate here you know we have to warn them about the perils of going back telling them telling them that there's really nothing to go back to that the old reality you know the one they existed in before all these reframes and these new beliefs we've inserted is a burning inferno that's about to crumble to the ground at any second and there's only really an illusion of going backwards you know it sounds dramatic and obviously your wording of this will be very different of whether or not you're selling something that's literally life for death or you know lessened inherently like language skills for example but if you're not willing to ring that fire alarm how do you expect your prospect to you know your job is still to find the life or death moment of that situation that moment of highest tension that we spoke about before there's always going to be a burning fire in any market and your job is to communicate that communicate the urgency of that and have them take that simple step out of it but the other thing we want to say is that Rome is still burning but it's been burning for a while you know a second element is that the fire didn't just start the moment you showed up on the scene with your product launcher your funnel you know you actually want to show them that they've been trying to solve this problem or achieve this outcome for a long time and by extending the timeline previous to their encounter with you you get them to notice that their desire is genuine it's not something manufactured just within the scope of your launch so what we're going to be doing here is we're extending the consistency principle into the indefinite past you know showing them how they've committed to that result forever and now with your help they're on to cusp of it you know all roads have led to this moment which really amplifies a sense of completion vindication and redemption which is that culmination of the buyer's journey if we could get it here you know and it's that Andy Dufresne moment again the ending of Shawshank and it it doesn't have to be as climatic as you know being kind of like into rain and like arms flailing and all that but it's powerful it's powerful and why was that ending in Shawshank so powerful um you know Andy Dufresne spent like over a decade in jail for a crime he didn't commit so it's over an injustice and the greater the injustice the more redeeming it's going to be so that's really the sense of completion we want to give your prospect and in the pre-launch we've absolved them of guilt you know so that makes them innocent innocent to the pain and struggle which becomes that injustice you know the market your competitors the sacred cows you've slaughtered in the pre-launch have become the oppressors you know the warden and then by showing them that the time they've been serving you know has extended well beyond when you came along that's when we get that amplified sense of vindication so you know that's what we're communicating here so Rome is burning Rome's been burning for a while and maybe it's been burning forever you know and with all that said if they can't go back into the burning inferno or the jail cell or whatever metaphor you want to use and can only go forwards into safety into that new future into that new step it's your job to frame it that way and it's your job to actually make that step as natural but necessary as possible you know make that step out of the fire as easy as possible and you know I like to kind of frame this who's more willing to escape the fire you know the person who just has to take a small step onto flat land or the person who has to jump out of a second-story window you know it's your job to give them that easy soft cushion to land on and think about that tipping point I showed you a few minutes ago our pre-launch already took them through the tough part the confrontational like breathing through the fire and escaping those flames and they made it and now they just need to claim the prize claim the rewards of that journey so we strategically made them take the most confronting and challenging part of the journey during that time of time of least friction without the offer and now that there is friction in taking out a credit card we leverage the same principle by offering both a small step and inviting them to ride that momentum and you could think of a car that hits friction it's kind of like driving through thick snow and if you just have a much better chance to get through it if you're using momentum and speed than if you stop so when we get to the workbook and the theme plates I'm going to walk you through a really effective and simple way of doing this to get buy-in and commitment to that next step actually right before you open the fire escape and ask them to step out another thing you're coaching here is you're coaching against procrastination there are a lot of benefits in getting people to buy right away when you open your car first and foremost you know you can get cash flow to reinvest in ads and retargeting isn't it a lot nicer to just get money in right away that's one thing we want to do it also gives you confidence confidence that people actually want your product that it's validated that it's working that your launch is actually succeeding your marketing is actually working you know these are very important and important signs especially mid-launch it just gives you more confidence to continue marketing to maybe reinvest in those ads and it also gives your partner's confidence if you have affiliates and partners mailing out for you to show that you're converting that's a big deal and that can make a big big difference in the long run if they see right off the bat that wow this is really working people really want it so it definitely pays to get a lot of sales right off the bat and sure we are still going to see the bulk of our sales in those closing hours that's not going to change but if we could just get momentum right out of the gates that's really going to be a big deal for us another thing we're doing is we're setting up the sale for later and one way to do this which you'll see in the theme plates is in creating a fast action bonus and this actually works on two levels because on one hand you're encouraging people who are ready to buy to well buy but on another level it gives you a chance to not just tell but show your other prospects the tangible cost of inaction you know for those that miss out on a fast action bonus at the beginning they'll see right away that there's a real cost in waiting so it starts to dissuade inaction you know they really get to kind of see what's going on here so by giving your prospects a taste of FOMO in a cart close light scenario with that fast action bonus they actually get acquainted with the pain of missing out and it's still fresh enough in their mind so that a few days later when you're going to pull the plug again they're less likely to miss out you know the lesson is still fresh in their mind and you know it's kind of like their hand is still burning from the last time they touched the stove like they just didn't like that feeling so it's a cart close light consider it really kind of a dress rehearsal for your final clothes so that by the time you get to the end of your sequence they're just already familiar with the pain of missing out and they know what to do to avoid it this time and this is what really coaches action later on you know how many of you have missed an opportunity felt the FOMO and then vowed to never let that happen again and that's what we're trying to achieve yes it's going to bring more buyers in right away but it's also to sell them against procrastination later in the sequence and this is also just the right way to add legitimate urgency at this stage you know I mentioned earlier how the biggest mistake is trying to create urgency around a three or five day cart close deadline which actually has the counter effect you know having a fast action bonus is where you get to leverage actual urgency while adding an extra incentive to ride that momentum through the right way so what types of fast action bonuses might you consider here and there are three types that I would usually work with here one is something that can can help them achieve the outcome quicker it has to have real world value and I think non scalable really works here so if there's you know any spots that leverages that involves your one-on-one time or the time of your team those are things that work really well as a quick action bonus because it's authentic scarcity you know there is a real hard limit on that and that is something that is valuable people do value your time and just having that kind of non scalable aspect to it makes the scarcity there feel real makes the urgency around it feel real instead of forced so that's what I'd like to use there that's usually what I recommend it could even be like a ticket to a limited capacity live event you know if you're hosting an intimate gathering for people in your program you could do that or once again just access to your team's time coaching group coaching quick start implementation sessions all that works really well at this stage and finally the last thing I really want to mention before we get into the workbook and the theme plays is this you know many of us have been trained to build hype and treat our launch as this big deal you know and we have a grand reveal and start talking about and only about how maybe it's been a labor of love for eons how our pets now hate us how this is our life's magnum opus and it's all about us and by all means be proud of what went into it and what you sacrifice to do it and inspire trust in your audience by letting them know that what they're getting is really a distillation of years and years and years of research work experience and investment but just remember the big deal isn't the fact that you've created this thing it's that they're now able to use it to vindicate and free themselves and I'm going to say that one more time because it's really the most important takeaway but the big deal isn't the fact that you spent all this time all these years crafting the perfect program and pouring your heart soul blood tears fluids whatever into this it's really that they are now able to take it and use it to vindicate themselves and free themselves from all the past previous attempts around solving this problem so that's the big deal and I really want you to internalize that when writing this launch email because it's so tempting to make it about us and our process because I know how hard you worked to create your offer I know how much went into it and I know you want your audience to know about all that and I'm not saying avoid that completely I'm just saying it's all about where you put the focus and the big deal is what they are now able to achieve the outcomes they're able to now realize with your help so on that note I'm going to jump out and I'm going to meet you back in the workbook in just a second where we're going to break down some questions and some theme plates to help you execute this really important mini sequence within your launch so I'll see you there in a bit
